using-imports-and-helper-files.label: Bati yon Aplikasyon Python ki gen plizyè fichye
using-imports-and-helper-files.summary: Kreye fichye modil yo andedan yon dosye, enpòte pwòp fonksyon ou yo nan main.py, epi konekte moso yo pou fè yon ti aplikasyon.

sketch0.title: Aplikasyon ou an ka genyen plis pase yon fichye.

sketch1.title: Enpòte soti nan yon fichye ki nan yon dosye

q9.title: Kreye yon modil pou netwaye non

q1.title: Poukisa divize kòd nan fichye?
q1.prompt: Poukisa yon pwojè Python ta mete fonksyon ki ka reitilize nan fichye tankou `tools/names.py` olye pou mete chak liy nan `main.py`?
q1.hint: Panse pou kenbe aplikasyon prensipal la fasil pou li epi reitilize menm lojik la pita.

help.concept: Yon fichye modil ka gen fonksyon ki ka itilize ankò. `main.py` ka rete konsantre sou koule aplikasyon an: li antre, rele fonksyon, epi enprime rezilta yo.
help.hint_1: Chèche opsyon sou òganizasyon ak reyitilizasyon.
help.hint_2: Objektif la se pa pou fè plis travay. Objektif la se pou kenbe chak fichye responsab pou yon travay klè.

options.a: Li kenbe kòd ki ka itilize ankò òganize epi pèmèt`main.py` enpòte li.
options.b: Sa fè Python inyore erè ki nan fichye enpòte a.
options.c: Sa vle di pwojè a ka sèlman fonksyone ak yon sèl valè antre.
options.d: Li anpeche fonksyon yo retounen valè

q2.title: Chwazi enpòtasyon dirèk la
q2.prompt: Ou kreye `tools/names.py` ak yon fonksyon ki rele `clean_name`. Ki liy ki enpòte fonksyon sa a nan `main.py`?
q2.hint: Chemen an itilize non dosye a, apre sa non fichye a san `.py`, epi non fonksyon an.

help.concept: Pou enpòte soti nan yon modil ki nan yon dosye, itilize `from folder.file import function`. Pou `tools/names.py`, chemen modil la se `tools.names`.
help.hint_1: Pa mete `.py` nan liy enpòtasyon an.
help.hint_2: Fòm kòrèk la kòmanse ak `from tools.names import ...`.

q3.title: Bon responsablite modil
q3.prompt: Chwazi tout egzanp ki bon travay pou yon ti fichye modil nan leson sa a.
q3.hint: Chwazi travay ki ka reitilize epi ki byen konsantre.

help.concept: Bon fichye modil yo anjeneral gen fonksyon ki gen rapò, tankou netwayaj tèks, fòmataj, evalyasyon, oswa kreyasyon rapò.
help.hint_1: Yon modil ta dwe gen yon objektif klè.
help.hint_2: Evite chwazi mete kòd o aza ki pa gen rapò nan yon sèl fichye.

options.b: `tools/badges.py` Bati tèks badj la apati yon non ak yon wòl.
options.c: `tools/random_everything.py` Melanje kòd ki pa gen rapò san okenn travay klè.
options.d: `main.py` Li dwe kopye tout fonksyon ki nan chak modil anvan li ka fonksyone.

q4.title: Apèl enpòte ki valab
q4.prompt: Ki apèl ki valab apre enpòtasyon ki koresponn lan? Chwazi tout repons ki kòrèk.
q4.hint: Koresponn stil apèl la ak stil enpòtasyon an.

help.concept: Apre `from tools.names import clean_name`, rele `clean_name(...)` dirèkteman. Apre `import tools.badges as badges`, rele `badges.make_badge(...)`.
help.hint_1: Enpòtasyon dirèk yo itilize non fonksyon an dirèkteman.
help.hint_2: Enpòtasyon alias yo itilize alias la anvan pwen an.

options.a: Apre `from tools.names import clean_name`, itilize `clean_name(" ava ")`
options.c: Apre `from tools.names import clean_name`, itilize `tools.names.clean_name("Ava")` san enpòte `tools`

q5.title: Kòmande modil workflow la
q5.prompt: Mete etap sa yo nan lòd ou ta itilize lè w ap ajoute pwòp fichye modil ou nan yon ti aplikasyon.
q5.hint: Kreye fichye a anvan ou enpòte ladan l.

help.concept: Python ka enpòte yon modil sèlman apre fichye a egziste nan chemen katab ki espere a. Apre sa,`main.py` ka rele fonksyon ki nan fichye sa a.
help.hint_1: Dosye a ak fichye a vini anvan enpòtasyon an.
help.hint_2: Apèl fonksyon an fèt apre liy enpòtasyon an.

tokens.t3: Defini yon fonksyon andedan fichye modil sa a.
tokens.t4: Enpòte epi rele fonksyon an ki soti nan `main.py`

q6.title: Bati koule enpòtasyon an
q6.prompt: Aranje liy sa yo pou`main.py` enpòte yon fòmatè epi enprime rezilta retounen an.
q6.hint: Enpòte an premye, apre sa rele, epi enprime.

help.concept: Yon fichye prensipal anjeneral enpòte fonksyon li bezwen an, rele li ak done, epi enprime oswa sere valè li retounen an.
help.hint_1: Liy enpòtasyon an dwe kouri anvan apèl fonksyon an.
help.hint_2: Enprime mesaj ki estoke a apre fonksyon an fin retounen li.

q7.title: Konplete enpòtasyon fonksyon an
q7.prompt: Ranpli non fonksyon ki manke a pou enpòte li soti nan `tools.names`.
q7.hint: Sa a se fonksyon ki netwaye espas sipèfl ak kapitalizasyon.

help.concept: `from tools.names import clean_name` Enpòte fonksyon `clean_name` la ki soti nan fichye `names.py` la ki nan dosye `tools` an.
help.hint_1: Mo ki manke a se non fonksyon an, pa non dosye a.
help.hint_2: Chwazi non ki dekri netwayaj yon non.

q8.title: Konplete chemen modil la
q8.prompt: Ranpli non dosye ki manke a nan liy enpòtasyon sa a.
q8.hint: Fichye modil pou pwojè sa a yo nan yon sèl dosye.

help.concept: Chemen enpòte `tools.badges` vle di Python ta dwe chèche `badges.py` andedan katab `tools`.
help.hint_1: Pa tape `.py` nan chemen enpòtasyon an.

q9.title: Kreye yon modil pou netwaye non
q9.prompt:
Pwojè sa a ap kòmanse yon ti pake èd. Kreye yon dosye ki rele `tools`, ajoute `tools/names.py`, epi defini `clean_name(text)` ladan l. Apre sa, enpòte `clean_name` ki soti nan `tools.names` nan `main.py`, netwaye non antre a, epi enprime l.

Pa egzanp, si antre a se `aVA`, pwogram ou an ta dwe enprime:
`Ava`.
q9.hint: Chemen ki nesesè a se `tools/names.py`. Liy enpòtasyon an nan `main.py` ta dwe `from tools.names import clean_name`.

help.concept: Etap pwojè sa a se pwogresif: kòd demaraj la se yon eta pwojè ki estab plis yon nouvo travay pou konplete.
help.hint_1: Pa efase kòd ki ap fonksyone nan etap anvan an; ajoute nouvo konpòtman ki mande nan etap sa a.
help.hint_2: Mete pwogram nan an kouri epi konpare sòti a ak egzanp ki te atann lan anvan ou verifye repons ou.

q9.starterCode:
# Create a folder named tools.
# Inside it, create tools/names.py.
# Define clean_name(text) in that file.

raw_name = input()

# Import clean_name from tools.names.
# Print the cleaned name.

main_py.content:
from tools.names import clean_name

raw_name = input()
print(clean_name(raw_name))

tools_init_py.content: # Tools package for this mini app.

2.message: clean_name ta dwe retire espas vid yo.

3.message: clean_name ta dwe mete non an an majiskil tit.

q9.solutionCode:
from tools.names import clean_name

raw_name = input()
print(clean_name(raw_name))

q10.title: Ajoute yon modil badj
q10.prompt:
Dosye èd la bezwen yon zouti anplis. Kreye '`tools/badges.py`', enpòte '`make_badge`' ki soti nan '`tools.badges`', netwaye non antre a, konstwi tèks badj la, epi enprime l.

Pa egzanp, si antre yo se `lina` ak `artist`, pwogram ou an ta dwe enprime:
`ARTIST badge: Lina`.
q10.hint: Kreye `tools/badges.py`, epi enpòte ak `from tools.badges import make_badge`.

help.concept: Etap pwojè sa a se pwogresif: kòd demaraj la se yon eta pwojè ki estab plis yon nouvo travay pou konplete.
help.hint_1: Pa efase kòd ki ap fonksyone nan etap anvan an; ajoute nouvo konpòtman ki mande nan etap sa a.
help.hint_2: Mete pwogram nan an fonksyònman epi konpare sòti a ak egzanp ki te atann lan anvan ou verifye repons ou.

q10.starterCode:
from tools.names import clean_name

raw_name = input()
role = input()

# Create tools/badges.py.
# Import make_badge from tools.badges.
# Clean the name, build the badge, and print it.

tools_init_py.content: # Tools package for this mini app.

main_py.content:
from tools.names import clean_name
from tools.badges import make_badge

raw_name = input()
role = input()
name = clean_name(raw_name)
print(make_badge(name, role))

tools_init_py.content: # Tools package for this mini app.

tools_badges_py.content:
def make_badge(name, role):
    return f"{role.upper()} badge: {name}"

2.message: make_badge ta dwe mete wòl la an majiskil.

q10.solutionCode:
from tools.names import clean_name
from tools.badges import make_badge

raw_name = input()
role = input()
name = clean_name(raw_name)
print(make_badge(name, role))

q11.title: Bati yon modil rapò misyon
q11.prompt:
Kounye a, konekte modil èd yo pou fè yon sèl rapò final. Kreye yon '`tools/reports.py`', enpòte yon '`mission_report`' ki soti nan '`tools.reports`', epi enprime rapò misyon ki fini an.

Pa egzanp, si antre yo se `zoe`, `scout`, ak `18`, pwogram ou an ta dwe enprime:
`Mission report: SCOUT badge: Zoe has 18 points`.
q11.hint: Etap sa a konbine twa fichye: `tools/names.py`, `tools/badges.py`, ak nouvo `tools/reports.py` ou a.

help.concept: Etap pwojè sa a se pwogresif: kòd demaraj la se yon eta pwojè ki estab plis yon nouvo travay pou konplete.
help.hint_1: Pa efase kòd ki ap fonksyone nan etap anvan an; ajoute nouvo konpòtman ki mande nan etap sa a.
help.hint_2: Kouri pwogram nan epi konpare rezilta a ak egzanp ki atann lan anvan ou tcheke repons ou.

q11.starterCode:
from tools.names import clean_name
from tools.badges import make_badge

raw_name = input()
role = input()
points = int(input())

# Create tools/reports.py.
# Import mission_report from tools.reports.
# Print the final mission report.

tools_init_py.content: # Tools package for this mini app.

tools_badges_py.content:
def make_badge(name, role):
    return f"{role.upper()} badge: {name}"

main_py.content:
from tools.names import clean_name
from tools.badges import make_badge
from tools.reports import mission_report

raw_name = input()
role = input()
points = int(input())
name = clean_name(raw_name)
badge = make_badge(name, role)
print(mission_report(badge, points))

tools_init_py.content: # Tools package for this mini app.

tools_badges_py.content:
def make_badge(name, role):
    return f"{role.upper()} badge: {name}"

tools_reports_py.content:
def mission_report(badge, points):
    return f"Mission report: {badge} has {points} points"

q11.solutionCode:
from tools.names import clean_name
from tools.badges import make_badge
from tools.reports import mission_report

raw_name = input()
role = input()
points = int(input())
name = clean_name(raw_name)
badge = make_badge(name, role)
print(mission_report(badge, points))

1. Kreye `tools/names.py`.
2. Nan `tools/names.py`, defini `clean_name(text)`.
3. Nan `main.py`, enpòte `clean_name` avèk `from tools.names import clean_name`.
4. Li yon non avèk `input()`, netwaye l, epi enprime rezilta a.

help.hint_2: Nan `main.py`, enpòte `clean_name`, rele li ak done brit yo, epi enprime non ki netwaye a.

try-using-imports-and-helper-files-sketc.prompt:

2. Nan `tools/badges.py`, defini `make_badge(name, role)`.
3. Retounen `ROLE badge: Name` avèk wòl la an lèt majiskil.

try-using-imports-and-helper-files-sketc.prompt:

1. Kreye `tools/names.py` ak `clean_name(text)`.
2. Kreye `tools/badges.py` ak `make_badge(name, role)`.
3. Nan `main.py`, enpòte toude èd yo.
4. Netwaye `"  mia  "`.

help.concept: Kat sa a pratike tout modèl la: fichye ede yo fè travay la, epi k`main.py`e yo ansanm.
help.hint_1: Itilize `from tools.names import clean_name` ak `from tools.badges import make_badge`.
help.hint_2: Netwaye `"  mia  "`, pase rezilta a nan `make_badge(..., "mentor")`, epi enprime badj la.

sk1.title: Aplikasyon ou an ka gen plis pase yon fichye.
sk1.bodyMarkdown:
A Python project does not have to keep every function in `main.py`. When a program grows, it is cleaner to move focused functions into small module files.

For this lesson, imagine a tiny **Mission Control** app:

```text
main.py
tools/
  names.py
  badges.py
  reports.py
```

Each file has a job:

- `main.py` reads input and connects the pieces.
- `tools/names.py` cleans names.
- `tools/badges.py` builds badge text.
- `tools/reports.py` formats the final report.

The folder name and file name become the import path. A function inside `tools/names.py` can be imported like this:

```python
from tools.names import clean_name
```

Notice that the import uses `tools.names`, not `tools/names.py`. Python import paths use dots and leave off `.py`.

In the project, you will create module files in a folder and import your own functions into `main.py`.

sk2.title: Enpòte soti nan yon fichye ki nan yon dosye
sk2.bodyMarkdown:
Suppose you create this file:

```text
tools/names.py
```

Inside `tools/names.py`, you can define a function:

```python
def clean_name(text):
    return text.strip().title()
```

Then `main.py` can import and use it:

```python
from tools.names import clean_name

raw_name = input()
name = clean_name(raw_name)
print(name)
```

Step by step:

1. Python looks inside the `tools` folder.
2. It finds the `names.py` module.
3. It imports the `clean_name` function.
4. `main.py` calls that function like any other function.

This keeps your main app clean. Instead of filling `main.py` with every detail, you give each module a focused job.

sk3.title: Fè yon ti klasè zouti
sk3.bodyMarkdown:
A nice multi-file project is not just random files. Each module should have a clear purpose.

Example:

```text
tools/
  names.py     # clean and format names
  badges.py    # build badge labels
  reports.py   # format final messages
```

The files can work together through imports:

```python
from tools.names import clean_name
from tools.badges import make_badge
from tools.reports import mission_report

name = clean_name("  zoe  ")
badge = make_badge(name, "scout")
print(mission_report(badge, 18))
```

Output:

```python
Mission report: SCOUT badge: Zoe has 18 points
```

This is the main idea of the project: create small module files, put reusable functions inside them, import those functions into `main.py`, and connect them into one clean app.
